subject: Social Science (History)
chapter: The Making of the National Movement: 1870s–1947

activities_projects.title: Critical Analysis of Early Nationalist Thought
activities_projects.objective: Analyze how early nationalist leaders criticized British economic policies
steps: Read about Dadabhai Naoroji and his Drain Theory
Identify economic criticisms made by early nationalists
Write a short analysis explaining how British rule affected India’s economy
Discuss how economic arguments strengthened nationalist ideas
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand economic nationalism and its role in shaping the freedom movement
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of economic analysis and clarity of explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Historical Source Interpretation
activities_projects.objective: Develop skills in interpreting historical speeches and writings
steps: Read excerpts from speeches or writings of leaders like Bal Gangadhar Tilak or Gopal Krishna Gokhale
Identify the main ideas expressed in the text
Explain how these ideas influenced political awareness
Write a short interpretation of the source
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn how historical writings shaped political thought in colonial India
activities_projects.evaluation: Interpretation skills and historical understanding (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Comparative Study of Moderates and Extremists
activities_projects.objective: Critically compare the political ideologies and methods of moderates and extremists
steps: List major leaders from both groups
Compare their political demands and methods
Explain the causes of the split in the Congress in 1907
Write a short comparative analysis
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand ideological differences within the national movement
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of comparison and analytical reasoning (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Economic Impact of the Swadeshi Movement
activities_projects.objective: Examine how the Swadeshi Movement influenced Indian industries
steps: Study the economic goals of the Swadeshi Movement
Identify industries encouraged by Swadeshi policies
Prepare a chart showing the relationship between boycott and industrial growth
Explain your findings in class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students analyze how economic resistance became a political strategy
activities_projects.evaluation: Analytical depth and clarity of presentation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Gandhi’s Political Strategies Case Study
activities_projects.objective: Evaluate the strategies used by Mahatma Gandhi during mass movements
steps: Study the Non-Cooperation, Civil Disobedience, and Quit India movements
Identify the goals and outcomes of each movement
Analyze why nonviolent protest became effective
Write a short case study explaining the impact of Gandhian methods
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the strategic role of nonviolent resistance
activities_projects.evaluation: Analytical reasoning and clarity of explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Impact Analysis of the Jallianwala Bagh Massacre
activities_projects.objective: Examine how the event transformed Indian public opinion against British rule
steps: Read detailed accounts of the Jallianwala Bagh massacre
Identify its immediate consequences
Explain how it influenced later political movements
Write a critical reflection on its significance
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the emotional and political impact of the event
activities_projects.evaluation: Historical reasoning and depth of reflection (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Mapping the Spread of the National Movement
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the geographical expansion of nationalist activities
steps: Mark key locations such as Champaran, Amritsar, Dandi, and Mumbai
Identify the movements associated with each place
Write brief notes explaining the significance of each event
Discuss how these events show the spread of nationalism
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the nationwide expansion of the freedom struggle
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of mapping and analytical explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Colonial Policy Evaluation Essay
activities_projects.objective: Critically evaluate a British colonial policy and its impact
steps: Choose a policy such as the Rowlatt Act or salt tax
Explain why the policy was introduced
Analyze its impact on Indian society
Write an essay evaluating why it caused resistance
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop critical thinking about colonial governance
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical argument and historical accuracy (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Freedom Movement Leadership Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Evaluate the contributions of different leaders in shaping the national movement
steps: Choose two freedom fighters with different approaches
Study their political ideas and methods
Compare their contributions to the national movement
Present your analysis in class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the diversity of leadership in the freedom struggle
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of research and comparative analysis (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Critical Reflection on Independence
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the long-term significance of India’s independence in 1947
steps: Reflect on the major movements that led to independence
Identify the role of mass participation in the struggle
Analyze the challenges faced after independence
Write a reflective essay discussing the historical significance of independence
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand independence as the result of a complex historical process
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of analysis and clarity of reflection (10 marks)
